PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Oct-11-2016 Continued To Nov-29-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Janet Boessenecker for the San Francisco Public Administrator. Scott Rahn (via Court Call) for Jessica Williams.

The Court stated that an Order to Appear was issued to Ms. Williams, that there is an amended report of status and a motion to be relieved as counsel, that the Public Administrator was invited to look at this case and that the Court believes that there are assets. Mr. Rahn stated that he has had no contact with Ms. Williams and that she is non responsive. Ms. Boessenecker reported that they have looked at the pleadings and inventory & appraisal, that they have talked to the Franchise Tax Board who is the largest creditor, that the estate looks to be insolvent, that per escrow the house was sold at a loss, that there is less than $49,000.00 in the trust account, that there is $82,000.00 in known liabilities and an absconding personal representative, that it makes most sense (citing 10953(c)(d)) to have the attorney do his best stab at an accounting, that the Court can figure out the surcharge and then pay off the attorneys and the creditors. Mr. Rahn stated that he appreciates the suggestion but does not have the information to complete an accounting. The Court stated that no one else would have more information and that something has to happen to close this estate. Mr. Rahn stated that he will put his best effort forth to do an accounting but that it will not be verified. The Court stated that it does not know where else to go, that the estate appears to have assets and asked what is the other claim besides the FTB. Ms. Boessenecker responded that it is the Department of Heath Services. Mr. Rahn stated that they had hoped to get the tax returns done. Ms. Boessenecker stated that notice will be given to the beneficiaries and that it may give them incentive to help. The Court stated that they received notice of the Order to Appear but did not come forward, that it will continue the matter for whatever counsel can do to close the estate, that it will continue the other matters to that date as well including the Order to Appear and Motion to be relieved as counsel. All matters were continued to November 29, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Rahn asked if the accounting petition could be heard on that date as well and was told by the Court that it could as long as it was filed in time for notice.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Grimaldi. (Not Reported)

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Sep-13-2016 Continued To Oct-11-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Scott Rahn for Jessica Williams (by CourtCall). Jessica Williams failed to appear in response to the Order to Appear.

The Court asked Mr. Rahn if he had had any further contact with Ms. Williams. Mr. Rahn replied that he had not; his client has ceased contact with him and failed to respond to his emails and other attempts to communicate with her. The Court inquired if Mr. Rahn had made any contact with the Public Administrator. Mr. Rahn responded that he had not as yet, but that considering the size of the estate, this may be something the Public Administrator could handle. The Court ordered the suspension of the powers of Jessica Williams to act as administrator for the estate of Melvin Lee Williams, and stated that he would issue a new order to appear for Ms. Williams to address her potential removal as administrator of the estate. The hearings on the status of petition for accounting and the motion to be relieved as counsel were continued to October 11, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court directed Mr. Rahn to invite the Public Administrator to attend the continued hearing.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Chan. (Not reported)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel-Civil

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Aug-09-2016 Continued To Sep-13-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:

Appearances: No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

An Order to Appear will be issued to the Administrator and the Attorney to appear on September 13, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to explain why the Accounting Status Report has not been filed and why no one appeared at today's hearing. The status hearing is also continued to that date. The Court further ordered that the hearing on the pending motion for leave to withdraw as counsel in this matter be continued from September 12 to September 13, 2016 at 9:00 am in Department 204 so that the matters may be heard together.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Grimaldi. (Not reported)

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Jul-12-2016 Continued To Aug-09-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Stefan O'Grady (via court call) for Jessica Williams.

The Court stated we are here on an amended report seeking additional time, that issues have been raised by the probate examiner but not addressed and that they need to be addressed. Mr. O'Grady stated that they were unable to address the issues raised, that they are having difficulties with cooperation from their client, that they are trying to deal with the outstanding tax issues and that at this point they need to either substitute out or withdraw as counsel. The Court asked if they have been able to negotiate with the tax authorities. Mr. O'Grady responded that the tax authorities want certain documents in order to proceed, that it will then take 6 months, that they have been unable to obtain the documents from their client, that they are unable to reach her, that they need to either substitute out or file a petition to be withdrawn and that they will do whatever the court wants. The Court stated that it cannot go forward without a response to the examiner's questions, that they also need an accounting, that something will have to be done and encouraged counsel to try and get in touch with their client. The accounting status report was continued to August 9, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not Reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Mar-30-2016 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Stefan O'Grady for (administrator) Jessica Williams.

Mr. O'Grady reported that they filed the status petition. The Court stated that the hearing on that status is set for May 2, 2016 and that the status on close of estate is off calendar.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Mar-16-2016 Continued To Mar-30-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Stefan O'Grady (via court call) for Jessica Williams. Mico Williams (beneficiary) in Propria Persona who arrived as the hearing was ending.

Mr. O'Grady stated that they are having the client execute a Power of Attorney so that they can get the tax transcripts and that they need a 60-90 day continuance to obtain the transcripts and prepare the petition for final distribution. The Court stated that it made it clear the last time that if the final petition was not filed they would need to file a petition for approval of status report and that they need to report to the court and to whoever else is involved what is going on. Mr. O'Grady stated that he will prepare a status and have it filed within 30 days. The Court stated that it should have been filed today, that it is not sure if there was no communication between Mr. O'Grady and Mr. Rahn or if he did not read the minutes from the last hearing, asked why it could not be filed in 2 weeks and that it understands why they need the additional time for the petition for final distribution. At this time Ms. Mico Williams arrived. Ms. Williams stated that she submitted a declaration, that she is in dire straits, that her and her son will be living on the streets, that she has medical issues, that her sister spent the money and liquidated assets but she is not sure how much, that they will not tell her anything, that she wants to start a business and can provide letters from the community about herself. The Court stated that Ms. Williams would need to get legal help, that the court cannot assist her, that it is keeping the pressure on to make sure the reports are filed, that the attorney for the administrator asked for a 90 day continuance and was given 2 weeks to file a report. Ms. Williams replied that the attorney and the administrator will not talk to her and that she submitted a declaration a month ago but no one read it. The status on close of the estate was continued to March 30, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Grimaldi. (Not Reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Feb-03-2016 Continued To Mar-16-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Scott Rahn (via court call) for Jessica Williams.

Mr. Rahn reported that they are not in a position to file the petition, that his predecessor fumbled, that they are currently trying to negotiate creditors claims, that the Franchise Tax Board will not negotiate until the tax returns are ready and requested 90 days to get the documents and tax returns completed. The status on close of estate was continued to March 16, 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for either a petition for final distribution or a petition to approve a status report.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel-Civil
Off Calendar Sep-09-2015 Continued To Oct-26-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Peter Myers for Myers Urbatsch. Jessica Williams in Propria Persona.

The Court asked for a status on Ms. Williams retention of new counsel. Ms. Williams reported that she lives in Long Beach and that she would prefer to find counsel there unless the court feels it is better to have a San Francisco attorney. The Court stated that it cannot move further until she has counsel and that Mr. Myers may need to stay on until that time. Ms. Williams stated that she will need 30 days to obtain counsel. The Court stated that if there is not new counsel in place at that time it will consider a suspension, that Ms. Williams could appear via court call if she has counsel here on her behalf or that if she retains counsel in Long Beach counsel can appear via court call. The notice of motion and motion to be relieved as counsel was continued to October 26, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)
